summary: Builder claimed to delete artifacts/gate_attempts.json but file still exists with stale 'rg' commands. Gate exit code is 1 because Lab Loop executes cached gate_attempts.json which uses ripgrep (rg), but rg is NOT INSTALLED on this system. config.env correctly uses 'grep' but is ignored. This is the THIRD cycle with the same root cause - potential stuck loop.

blockers.title: Builder's fix not applied - gate_attempts.json still exists with rg commands
blockers.details: Builder Report claims: 'rm /home/zaks/bookkeeping/labloop/tasks/deal_lifecycle_v3_remediation/artifacts/gate_attempts.json' was executed with exit 0. However, file still exists (verified: -rw-r--r-- 1 root root 4278 Feb 3 23:52) and contains 7 occurrences of 'rg' commands. The claimed deletion did NOT happen. This is either a command that was planned but not executed, or a file permission issue, or the file was recreated by Lab Loop infrastructure between Builder and QA phases.
blockers.expected: File should not exist, or should contain grep commands (matching config.env)
blockers.actual: File exists with timestamp Feb 3 23:52, contains 7 'rg' commands, ripgrep is not installed on system
blockers.recommended_fix: The Lab Loop infrastructure likely reads GATE_CMD at task initialization and caches it in gate_attempts.json. Deleting may not work if Lab Loop recreates it. INSTEAD: Edit gate_attempts.json directly and replace all 'rg' with 'grep' in the gate_cmd JSON field. Use sed or manual edit: sed -i 's/rg -q/grep -rq/g; s/rg -q/grep -q/g' artifacts/gate_attempts.json
validation_steps: Run the gate command and verify exit code 0

blockers.title: ripgrep (rg) not installed - cached gate command cannot execute correctly
blockers.details: The gate_attempts.json uses 'rg' commands but ripgrep is not installed on this system. 'which rg' returns empty, and 'rg --version' fails. All rg commands silently fail (exit non-zero or no matches), causing grep-for-pattern checks to incorrectly report failures.
blockers.expected: Either rg is installed, OR gate commands use grep instead of rg
blockers.actual: rg is NOT installed. Gate commands in gate_attempts.json use rg. config.env correctly uses grep but is being ignored.
blockers.recommended_fix: Either: (1) Install ripgrep: apt-get install ripgrep, OR (2) Edit gate_attempts.json directly to replace all 'rg' references with 'grep' equivalents. Option 2 is preferred as it matches config.env intent.
validation_steps: bash the gate_cmd from gate_attempts.json and verify it passes

minors.title: STUCK LOOP PATTERN: Same blocker for 3 consecutive cycles
minors.details: QA Memory shows Cycles 1-2 both failed with 2 BLOCKERs related to rg vs grep. This is now Cycle 3 with the same root cause. Builder in Cycle 2 fixed config.env. Builder in Cycle 3 claims to have deleted gate_attempts.json but it still exists. The loop is stuck on infrastructure issues, not V3 remediation issues.
repro_steps: Review QA_MEMORY: Cycle 1 FAIL (2B/0M/1m), Cycle 2 FAIL (2B/0M/1m)
minors.expected: Issues should be resolved after Builder addresses them
minors.actual: Same infrastructure issue persists across 3 cycles - file deletion not working
minors.recommended_fix: Builder should EDIT gate_attempts.json in-place rather than deleting it. Use: python3 -c "import json; f=open('artifacts/gate_attempts.json'); d=json.load(f); d['gate_cmd']=d['gate_cmd'].replace('rg -q','grep -rq').replace('rg ','grep '); open('artifacts/gate_attempts.json','w').write(json.dumps(d,indent=2))"
validation_steps: Verify next cycle doesn't have the same blocker

next_actions_for_builder.summary: EDIT gate_attempts.json IN-PLACE to replace 'rg' with 'grep' (do NOT delete the file)
how_to_validate: Read gate_attempts.json and verify it contains grep commands not rg commands
grep -c 'grep' artifacts/gate_attempts.json should return 7 or more
Run: bash -c '<gate_cmd from gate_attempts.json>' and verify exit code 0

next_actions_for_builder.summary: After editing, verify the actual gate command passes when executed

Independent verification with grep commands PASSES: 'grep -q LEGACY DECOMMISSION ...' returns 0
config.env correctly uses grep at lines 38, 49, 60, 68, 79, 90, 125 (but is being ignored)
QA Memory shows same pattern: Cycle 1 FAIL (2B), Cycle 2 FAIL (2B), Cycle 3 FAIL (2B) - STUCK LOOP
